programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: The first invasion of the Italian Campaign of WWII: Operation Husky.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: A secret British regiment in 1942 led a daring raid on a Nazi-occupied Libyan harbor.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: An elite squad of Norwegians destroy a power plant in 1943 that the Nazis may have been using for nuclear weapons research.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: SAS Flight 686 takes off from Milan's Linate Airport and moments later collides with a Cessna on the runway.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Indonesian investigators must determine why a Boeing 737 Max 8 -- one of the world's best-selling new airplanes -- plunged into the Java Sea.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: TAM flight 402 slammed into a residential neighborhood and burst into flames, just seconds after lifting off from Congonhas airport in São Paulo, Brazil, killing all 95 people on board and three people on the ground.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On December 21, 1992, Martinair Flight 495 crashes off the right side of the runway while attempting to land at Faro Airport in Faro, Portugal, in severe weather conditions, killing 56 of the 340 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: After losing two engines near enemy territory, U.S. Air Force pilots abort their refueling mission and try to land their damaged plane.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: An aerial tour over the places made famous by America's most influential men and women.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: A view of Idaho from the air reveals a state filled with agricultural fields, lands untouched by human hands, and riches in the form of gold, ski resorts and spuds.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: Louisiana's wetlands and underwater oil fields.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: A coast-to-coast aerial journey across America, visiting locations made famous by movies such as Rocky, Citizen Kane, Forrest Gump and others.

programme.desc: Explore the forests, shores, bridges and tunnels of one of American's 13 original states, New Jersey.

programme.desc: Home to some of America's highest mountains; Mount St. Helens.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: Natural wonders, manmade marvels, hidden treasures and places of inspiration.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: Viewing Oklahoma from the sky reveals a state filled with tall-grass prairies and man-made wonders; the Dust Bowl, Tornado Alley and buffalo-filled plains; it also shows a history steeped in tragedy and devastation.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: A flight over Arizona explores the state's storied past, architectural achievements and landscapes, including America's natural wonder, the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: An aerial tour over America's Great Plains tracks the prairies, farms, oilfields and wildlife.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: Vast cotton fields, Gulf coast beaches; famous Civil Rights marches; NASCAR's most dangerous track; steel factories.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: An aerial tour of Nevada; the state's deserts, ghost towns, neon cities and Hoover Dam; its history built on epic booms and busts.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Saudi Airways and Kazakhstan Airlines jets hit each other at 14,000 feet sending both planes spiraling to the ground.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine what caused the Zeebrugge ferry tragedy and the Costa Concordia cruise ship disaster; what were the consequences, and how could they have been avoided?

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine how engineering failures allowed Hurricane Katrina to devastate New Orleans and cause the collapse of the Tacoma Narrows Bridge.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Following three horrifying crashes, investigators uncover fatal flaws in the very operations designed to prevent aircraft from flying into mountains.

programme.desc: Experts examine how engineering failures allowed Hurricane Katrina to devastate New Orleans and cause the collapse of the Tacoma Narrows Bridge.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A plane carrying a Brazilian soccer team crashes just ten miles from the airport in 2016.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at three commercial planes that became targets over the Sinai Desert, the Sea of Japan and Ukraine.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: The FBI and NTSB join forces to determine how terrorists hijacked American Airlines Flight 77 and flew it into the Pentagon on September 11, 2001.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: TANS Perú Flight 204 crashes into a muddy swamp while attempting to land at Pucallpa Airport, Peru, in a thunderstorm, killing 40 of the 98 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A plane crash lands in Guam after losing sight of the airport in a storm.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A large cargo jet slices into an apartment complex in Amsterdam and reveals a potentially lethal problem threatening every 747 in the sky.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: How routine flights can turn catastrophic in mere seconds when the pilots become momentarily distracted; three flights end in a disaster.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Kobe Bryant dies when a high-tech helicopter crashes on a routine L.A. route-training ignored with deadly results; in Missouri, cockpit chatter dooms Corporate 5966; in Lexington, a twin-engine jet crashes after taking off from the wrong runway.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigators race to determine the cause of the deadliest single aircraft accident in history: the 1985 crash of Japan Airlines 123, which claimed 520 lives.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: With its conductors sound asleep, a freight train fails to stop and collides with an oncoming passenger train, resulting in tragedy.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 stalls and crashes less than a mile from the runway while approaching Amsterdam Airport Schiphol.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: In the skies above the Mojave Desert, the VSS Enterprise accelerates toward the outer reaches of Earth's atmosphere, guided by test pilots; the aircraft breaks apart and falls from the sky, killing the flight's co-pilot.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A simple error years prior causes the deadliest single-plane crash in history.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: The 2010 crash of Ethiopian Airlines Flight 409 sparks rumors of terrorism.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Arrow Air Flight 1285 with 248 American soldiers on board crashes seconds after refueling in Newfoundland.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A passenger plane veers off the runway in Denver sparking an investigation into the airport's proximity to the Rocky Mountains.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine how engineering failures allowed Hurricane Katrina to devastate New Orleans and cause the collapse of the Tacoma Narrows Bridge.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: An elite group of British paratroopers launch a midnight raid in order to capture Hitler's unusual radar equipment.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: American paratroopers are dropped behind German lines on D-Day in order to secure territory behind the beaches.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: British Commandos raid two occupied Norwegian islands to wipe out fish oil factories used to make explosives.

programme.desc: An elite squad of Norwegians destroy a power plant in 1943 that the Nazis may have been using for nuclear weapons research.
